title: An LLM Wiki
summary: A public, privacy-filtered Obsidian LLM Wiki rebuilt around Karpathy's llm-wiki pattern.
contentLines: This is a public-safe personal Obsidian knowledge base rebuilt around Andrej Karpathy's LLM Wiki pattern.
- [Kimi frontend reference](docs/kimi-frontend-reference.md) - public-safe product brief, data model, prompt patterns, and frontend acceptance criteria

Raw material stays local. Public pages are compiled, linked, and source-aware Markdown notes. Sensitive local paths, credentials, private memory, raw conversations, and unreviewed imports are excluded from the public repository.
goal: A public, privacy-filtered Obsidian LLM Wiki rebuilt around Karpathy's llm-wiki pattern.
audience: 希望沿着公开资料路线学习、复刻或维护知识库的读者。
finalOutput: 形成一条可复用的公开学习或构建路径。

title: Sources and Data Policy
summary: Source, privacy, and publication rules for this public-safe wiki.
contentLines: ## Public By Default Only After Filtering
Compiled wiki pages may be public when they contain no credentials, raw private logs, local environment maps, personal memory dumps, browser/session data, or true local filesystem paths.
- raw exports from chats, browsers, tools, or local applications
Run structural and privacy checks before committing or pushing.

toc.title: Public By Default Only After Filtering

title: GitHub Reference Projects
summary: Secondary engineering references for local-first LLM Wiki implementations.
contentLines: These projects inform implementation details. They do not replace Karpathy's gist as the architecture source.
- MehmetGoekce/llm-wiki - Claude Code and layered wiki workflow
- yologdev/karpathy-llm-wiki - web app and agentic experiment

title: Karpathy LLM Wiki Pattern
summary: Official pattern source for this vault architecture.
contentLines: The official architecture source for this vault is Andrej Karpathy's `llm-wiki.md` gist:
- raw material remains separate from compiled wiki pages
- the LLM maintains a persistent Markdown artifact
- Obsidian is the browsing and graph surface
- schema/rule files guide future agent maintenance

title: llm-wikid
summary: Secondary GitHub reference for Obsidian vault template and wiki maintenance conventions.
contentLines: This repository is a secondary implementation reference for Obsidian-oriented vault conventions. It does not replace Karpathy's `llm-wiki.md` gist as the official pattern source.
- Borrow only practical vault-template ideas that fit the public-safe structure.
- Keep `_raw/`, private inbox material, and volatile Obsidian state outside Git.
- Validate every publishable page through the local structure and privacy scripts.

title: obsidian-llm-wiki-local
summary: Secondary GitHub reference for local-first LLM Wiki implementation ideas.
contentLines: This repository is a secondary implementation reference for local-first operation ideas. It does not define the architecture standard for this vault.
- Treat it as an engineering reference for local CLI and local model workflows.
- Do not copy its structure over Karpathy's raw-source to compiled-wiki pattern.
- Prefer this vault's privacy gates and source notes when migrating content.

title: Pre-Rebuild Vault Archive
summary: Local private archive used as the source for public-safe migration.
contentLines: The previous vault was snapshotted before rebuild.
- Public repository policy: do not publish archive paths or raw private data.
- Use this source note to indicate that migrated pages were compiled from the private pre-rebuild vault after privacy filtering.
